topic: JetBrains AI Pulse Survey 2026: 85% of Developers Now Use AI

key_points: 85% of developers regularly use AI tools for coding and development
62% rely on at least one AI-powered coding assistant, agent, or code editor
Only 29% trust AI outputs to be accurate — a critical trust gap
GitHub Copilot leads with 76% awareness and 29% workplace adoption
January 2026 AI Pulse survey: 10,000+ professional developers in 8 languages
90% of developers use AI at work; 74% use specialized AI developer tools
GitHub Copilot 29%, Cursor 18%, Claude Code 18% — all tied for top 3
JetBrains AI Assistant 9%, Junie 5% usage rates
Claude Code CSAT 91% and NPS 54 — highest loyalty metrics on the market
Claude Code 6x growth in adoption from April-June 2025 to January 2026
Regular AI users produced 320% (4.2x) more durable code than non-users
AI redistributes developer workflows in ways that elude developers' own perception
Only 13% of developers use AI across the entire SDLC
Many developers do more but also spend 11.4 hrs/week reviewing AI-generated code vs 9.8 hrs writing new code
Productivity gains may come at cost of validation skills
GitHub Copilot growth in awareness and adoption has stalled since last year
Shift toward best-of-breed agents — product excellence over ecosystem lock-in
Google Antigravity reached 6% adoption by January 2026 after November launch
In US/Canada, Claude Code adoption reached 24% — strong in English-speaking markets
84% AI coding tool adoption overall in 2026
29% of developers trust AI-generated code accuracy
61% agree AI often produces code that looks correct but isn't reliable
Only 48% of developers always check AI-assisted code before committing
22% of developers already use AI coding agents; 66% of companies plan to adopt within 12 months

stats.fact: 85-90% of developers now regularly use AI tools for coding and development tasks
stats.source: JetBrains AI Pulse Survey, January 2026 (10,000+ developers, 8 languages)

stats.fact: 74% of developers worldwide have adopted specialized AI tools (coding assistants, editors, agents) — not just general chatbots
GitHub Copilot: 76% awareness, 29% used at work — most widely adopted but growth has stalled
Cursor and Claude Code both at 18% workplace usage — tied for second place
Claude Code grew 6x in adoption from April-June 2025 (~3%) to January 2026 (18%)
Claude Code CSAT 91%, NPS 54 — highest product loyalty metrics in the AI coding tool market
JetBrains AI Assistant used by 9% of developers; Junie by 5% worldwide
Only 29% of developers trust AI-generated code to be accurate
stats.source: JetBrains / Uvik AI Coding Assistant Stats 2026

stats.fact: 61% of developers agree AI often produces code that 'looks correct but isn't reliable'
Only 48% of developers always check AI-assisted code before committing
22% of developers already use AI coding agents; 66% of companies plan to adopt agents within 12 months
Only 13% of developers report using AI across the entire software development lifecycle
Regular AI users produced 320% (4.2x) more durable code than non-users and increased output by 25% from 2024 to 2025
stats.source: JetBrains AI Impact on Developer Workflows Study, April 2026

stats.fact: In US and Canada, Claude Code adoption reached 24% — indicating stronger English-market uptake
Google Antigravity (AI code editor, launched November 2025) reached 6% adoption by January 2026

angles: The trust gap: 85-90% use AI but only 29% trust it — what does this mean for code quality at scale?
GitHub Copilot's stalled growth vs Claude Code's 6x surge — what changed and why
The agentic shift: 22% already using agents, 66% companies planning adoption — how to prepare
Why only 13% use AI across the full SDLC — biggest barriers and how to overcome them
AI tool fragmentation: developers now use multiple tools (chatbots + specialized assistants + agents)
The 'almost right' problem: 61% say AI produces plausible-but-unreliable code — verification strategies
JetBrains' own position: AI Assistant 9%, Junie 5% — how they're responding with JetBrains Central
Geographic differences: Claude Code 24% in US/Canada vs 18% globally — market dynamics
keywords: github copilot vs cursor vs claude code
outline: H2: JetBrains AI Pulse Survey 2026: What It Is and Why It Matters
H2: Key Finding #1 — 85-90% of Developers Now Use AI (But Trust Lags Far Behind)
H2: Key Finding #2 — The AI Tool Market Share Battle: GitHub Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code
H2: Key Finding #3 — The Agentic Revolution Is Already Here (22% and Climbing)
H2: Key Finding #4 — AI Across the Full SDLC: The 13% Problem
H2: Key Finding #5 — Productivity Is Real (4.2x More Durable Code), But So Is the Verification Burden
H2: What JetBrains Is Doing About It: AI Assistant, Junie, and JetBrains Central
H2: What These Survey Results Mean for Developers in 2026
H2: How to Choose Your AI Stack Based on the JetBrains Data
